column7: Distancia do passo do teclado (com <CTRL> modificador).
Adicionar os movimentos da faca de arrasto
Porta está aberta, fechar porta e continuar?
Estado da porta não pode ser limpo com <Reset>
E-Stop ativo! - limpar antes de prosseguir...
Ciclo de homing necesário, <;Ínicio> para continuar
Controlador está no estado {0} e não pode responder, tentar reiniciar(soft reset)?
Não é possível se comunicar com o controlador devido ao alarme {0}, tentando reiniciar(soft reset).
Probe over the loaded job's XY extent, in the current work coordinates.
Probe the whole in-bounds machine travel envelope (referenced to the homed corner, like Surface spoilboard).
How far DOWN from the parked (start) Z each probe searches before it faults. Park Z at a small safe clearance above the surface, then this must be at least that clearance plus the surface variation.

Feed for every probe. The surface is already roughly known, so all points probe gently at this single approach feed - no fast search pass - which avoids overshoot/false triggers on a fragile probe. Defaults to the probe's latch (slow) feed.
Pause before each probe so a fragile/3D probe fully releases and stops ringing after the previous touch and the move. Raise this if you get 'probe already triggered' fails partway through. 0 = none.

Sacrificial board thickness under the stock (same footprint), e.g. 1/4 in MDF a thin sheet is taped to. 0 = none. The metal top is probed for Z0; this keeps the side probe on the material, not the backer.
Copy the measured stock size (X Y [Z] in mm) to the clipboard for Fusion's ioSenderBatchPost 'measured stock' field.
Probe all four corners to measure the true stock size and skew. When off, the width/height above are used as-is and only the front-left origin (and optional TLO reference) is set.
Store the measured stock skew as the work coordinate system's rotation (G10 L2 R), so any program run against this stock is cut aligned to it. Requires firmware with ROTATION_ENABLE.
Reference the tool length at the toolsetter puck (M6 T8, probe already in the spindle) after the origin is set - the core of a one-button job start. Requires ATC and a toolsetter at G59.3.
After a measure run: re-apply the measured origin + skew rotation, then touch each corner in the rotated work frame. If the skew is right (and the stock square) the probe lands right at every corner.
Open the per-user folder where ioSender keeps its settings (App.config), key mappings, grbl backups and restore points.
Copy the full status message to the clipboard
Load a folder of per-operation g-code files as one combined program.
Check if a newer version of ioSender is available on GitHub.
Show/hide the program view over the work area.
Demo capture: mark the timelapse window. Flip ON at the moment you switch the camera to timelapse, OFF when you switch back. Auto-ON a few minutes after Cycle Start if you don't; auto-OFF at job end.
Type a name and Set to save the current machine position as a fixture instance.
Store the current machine position as a fixture instance.

Touch plate only. Checked: the tool touches the conductive stock directly, no offset. Unchecked: a separate metal touch plate is used against non-conductive stock - its ProbeDefinition PlateThickness is subtracted from the probed Z.
Probes by electrical continuity - works on conductive stock directly, or on non-conductive stock via a separate metal touch plate (see 'Stock conductive' below).
Write a .nc file that calls an O-word subroutine several times, for reproducing controller-wedge-on-Reset bugs.
Swap back to the ioSender build installed before the last update. Only goes back one version - it does not work if you have not updated at least once.
The loaded program's own (STOCK X=.. Y=.. Z=..) comment differs from the values above - click to copy it in.

Corners 2-4 travel at corner 1's own measured stock top plus this margin, instead of retracting fully to machine top between corners. Must clear any fence/clamp hardware between corners on this fixture.

Off (default): width/height above are a conservative ESTIMATE - a few mm larger than actual - so far-corner references land safely outside the stock. On: they are treated as exact, and corners 2-4 probe close to their computed true position instead (corner 2 assumes near-zero skew; corners 3/4 use the skew measured from corners 1-2). Faster and tighter, but a wrong entered size can miss the stock - only enable if you trust the numbers.
Start probing the height map grid.
Cancel the height map probing in progress.
Rewrite the loaded program to follow the probed surface.
Save the height map to a file (.map) to reuse later.
Load a previously saved height map (.map) file.
Fixture to reference for this job (defined in Machine Setup Wizard > Fixture definitions).
Work coordinate system (G54-G59) to set this job's origin in.
